description: Fix three issues identified in semantic review: response body leak on HTTP error, no cooperative cancellation support, and progress emission flood.
steps: Wrap response handling with response.use { } to ensure the body is closed on all paths (including error)
Register OkHttp Call cancellation on coroutine completion using invokeOnCompletion, and add ensureActive() inside the read loop
Throttle progress emissions to only emit when integer percentage changes
acceptance_criteria: Response body is properly closed on error paths via response.use { }
OkHttp Call is cancelled when the coroutine is cancelled
ensureActive() is called inside the read loop for cooperative cancellation
Progress emissions are throttled to percentage-point changes (~100 emissions max for any file size)
New imports are added: kotlin.coroutines.coroutineContext, kotlinx.coroutines.ensureActive, kotlinx.coroutines.job
verification: Verify Kotlin syntax correctness of modified file
findings: Gradle build cannot run due to network timeout downloading Gradle distribution (same as FEAT-001). Code changes are syntactically correct Kotlin with proper use of coroutineContext, ensureActive(), job extension, and Response.use {} - all standard Kotlin/OkHttp patterns. No Kotlin compiler available in sandbox for standalone syntax verification.
